name: Alexia Andrews
about: Alexia enjoys exploring design at the intersection of fitness, travel, and art, transforming personal passions into visual communications. She is drawn to work that inspires action, using design as a tool to motivate and connect with people beyond the moment. Grounded in her own experiences, she aims to craft work that feels both engaging and meaningful.

name: Anna McFalls
about: Anna McFalls is a multidisciplinary creative with experience spanning graphic design, visual arts, advertising, and photography. Her past experiences focus on entertainment design, working with brands such as Disney and NBCUniversal. After graduation she will continue working towards the goal of becoming an art director within the entertainment space.

name: Danny Nguyen
about: Danny is a multidisciplinary designer specializing in video, motion, and branding. He helps brands build strong visual identities through dynamic, engaging visual storytelling. His work focuses on creating cohesive and impactful motion-driven experiences.

name: Del Halter
about: Del Halter is an information designer focused on positive social impact. They’re interested in how people absorb information, how physical and digital spaces are involved in this process, and how to improve information accessibility. In their free time, they enjoy writing and hiking.

name: Delaney Rosson
about: Delaney is a graphic designer with a mixed-media background who uses illustration and storytelling to create thoughtful, immersive visual identities. Her projects range from UI/UX to print, often merging digital precision with hands-on experimentation. In her free time, she enjoys trying new foods and traveling.

name: Emily Delice
about: Emily Delice learned how design informs our world from experienced professors and peers from all walks of life and the opportunities to create work for health, education, and entertainment. Her areas of expertise include branding, typography, and user interface design. After graduation, Emily hopes to continue looking for new ways to use knowledge of the past to inform design for the future

name: Isabella Inciarte
about: Isabella is a graphic designer focused on brand identity and content creation for both print and digital platforms. She is drawn to projects that explore visual systems and how they can reflect personality, emotion, and belonging.

name: Jake Velazquez
about: Jake is a graphic designer with an interest in creating publication, packaging, and merchandise designs. Jake always tries to incorporate his unique interests within his artworks, like his love of music and theme parks. In his free time, Jake enjoys playing on his Nintendo Switch 2!

name: Jazmin McShane
about: Jazmin McShane is a graphic designer whose work explores the expressive potential of color through bold yet balanced palettes. Her practice spans typography, illustration, and animation, blending technical skill with a strong visual sensibility. Drawing on both professional and volunteer experiences, she creates striking designs that bring together chaos and cohesion.

name: Josh Reimer
about: Josh Reimer is an illustrator, packaging designer, and marketer aiming to reshape how we think about the life cycle of product creation. He enjoys working with physical materials to shape how ideas develop. Through his work, Josh prompts interactions with the world in ways that highlight the strange, the absurd, and the fun.

name: Kayla Greer
about: Kayla Greer is a graphic designer who specializes in branding, illustration, marketing, and merch design. She is passionate about bringing concepts to life through bold, playful visuals and cute, character-driven mascots that fuse fun with purpose into distinctive brand identities. In her free time, you’ll find her browsing through oddities markets.

name: Macy McClarin
about: Macy McClarin is a graphic designer who is passionate about music, culture, and entertainment. She specializes in marketing and merchandise design for the music industry and spotlighting the LGBTQ+ community. In Macy’s free time, she plays bass guitar, bakes cookies for friends, and needle felts dolls of her favorite movie and tv characters.

name: Mads Kairalla
about: Mads Kairalla is a graphic designer who also studies advertising, so their work usually sits somewhere between visuals and strategy. Having a background in coding, engineering, and 3D printing, they love to innovate, overcomplicate, and hyperfixate. Their dream is to build things that exist out in the world, whether they're a brand systems, products, or advertisements.

name: Makenna Paul
about: Makenna Paul is a graphic designer who enjoys working with typography, branding, digital collaging and dabbles in photography. She is passionate about working with small businesses and hopes to run one of her own some day. In her free time, Makenna enjoys performing in musical theatre productions and traveling to new places!

name: Megan Williamson
about: Megan is a graphic designer specializing in branding, visual storytelling, and packaging design. She has experience creating work within higher education and startup businesses. Her work focuses on human-centered design, playful visual systems, and bold typography, and is rooted in a commitment to creating work that brings meaningful change to people’s lives.

name: Orionne Burbea
about: Orionne is a graphic designer driven by the infinite means by which visuals can elicit thought-provoking and impressionable art. She is interested in pursuing a career in editorial print design. She strives not only to further develop her technical skillset, but also challenge herself in the ways in which she can shape the ways people experience art and foster greater connections.

name: Rebekah Noonan
about: Rebekah is a thoughtful communicator and problem solver drawn to meaningful creative projects. Her designs are full of personality and playful charm while maintaining an empathetic focus on human connection. She works in a constant state of organized chaos with a mile-long to-do list, but always finds time to go for a walk, write lyrics, and add a few thousand photos to her camera roll.

name: Trinity Burnett
about: Trinity Burnett is a graphic designer who is drawn to packaging, branding, and illustration. She loves creating playful, engaging visuals, especially for child-centered audiences. In her free time she likes to make crafty projects and work on her scrapbook.

name: Alexa Gottlieb
about: Alexa Gottlieb is a graphic designer focused on packaging, apparel, and brand identity. Her work is rooted in bright colors and bold visuals, blending strategic thinking with storytelling. Alexa draws inspiration from everyday moments, documenting memories that support her visual language and deepen her connection to storytelling-driven design.

name: Cameron Relick
about: Cameron Relicke is a multidisciplinary creative director and graphic designer working across music, fashion, and editorial design. His style is bold, raunchy, and eye-catching, drawing inspiration from pop star branding and provocative visual storytelling. He specializes in magazine production, branding, and digital design.

name: Camila Gutierrez
about: Camila Gutierrez is a graphic designer interested in typography, branding, and trend-driven visual culture. Her work blends bold color with alternative influences like cybersigilism, creating designs both current and expressive. With an eye for evolving aesthetics, Camila approaches design as a space for experimentation and identity-building.

name: Daisy Armagost
about: Daisy is a designer who values thoughtful storytelling, strong visual systems, and creative collaboration. She is excited by opportunities that allow design to support culture, community, and entertainment in meaningful ways and help artists and creative organizations communicate their voices authentically.

name: Diana Perez
about: Diana Perez is a concept-driven graphic designer with a special interest in branding, pattern-making, and typography. Her research and dialogue-based process aims to balance form and function with her stylistic touch. When she isn’t at work, Diana channels her creative energy devising new songs and games for her parrot, and recently, practicing rollerskating.

name: Emily Murphy
about: Emily Murphy is a graphic designer with an interest in illustration, branding, typography, and motion design, all within social spaces. She brings playfulness and authenticity to the forefront of her work. Outside of design, Emily enjoys other creative activities, mainly dancing and drawing.

name: Emily Rosendo
about: Emily is a multi-hyphenate creative and enjoys working with good people on fun projects. She designs for brands and entertainment, with a focus on visual identity, print, and story driven work. And what’s more, also loves working across marketing campaigns, design systems, and narratives that connect people together in the art and culture space.

name: Gaby Carstens
about: Gabriella is a graphic designer creating active, engaging work across motion and physical spaces. Interested in environmental and exhibition design, she brings a versatile approach, balancing bold and colorful visuals with softer, more playful details that invite curiosity and new perspectives. Her process is heavily influenced by music and time spent outdoors, which keep her work grounded and evolving.

name: Jasmine Hickman
about: Jesus Christ and the hope of His good news form the foundation of Jasmine’s creative excellence. She creates work that is not only visually compelling but also purposeful, aiming to repel darkness, spread love, and usher in light. Through graphic design, Jasmine uses her skills to serve others while pursuing her calling to create for the glory of God.

name: Jessica Blackman
about: Jessica Blackman is a multidisciplinary graphic designer with specialties including branding, packaging, print, and advertising design. She has an eye for small details, designing with intention to create work that is well-structured, accessible, and organized while also being bright and fun. When not designing, she loves to crochet, spend time with her siblings, and going to the beach!

name: Marlie Hayes
about: Marlie is a designer that loves to make everything political and likes to think about our place in society. She loves the intentional use of typography and color in her designs. She hopes to create branding and educational designs to help mission driven organizations accomplish their goals.

name: Oskar Garcia
about: Oskar is a designer, illustrator, and bird enthusiast. His passion for the natural world is reflected through his work combining art and environmentalism. He is dedicated to conservation and often showcases nature in his design work using fun illustrations and vibrant colors to inspire awe in the natural world.

name: Rachel Frenchman
about: Rachel is a graphic designer who creates clear, story-driven work across branding, editorial, and visual design. Her approach is grounded in strong composition and typography, focusing on work that feels thoughtful, refined, and emotionally resonant. Outside of design, she draws inspiration from film and music, which continuously shape her creative perspective.

name: Sam Sonnenberg
about: Sam Sonnenberg is a graphic designer specializing in sports media, and system-based design. His approach combines bold typography, color, illustration, and structured design thinking to create work that is both expressive and functional. Beyond his work, Sam enjoys sitting down to watch a movie or going around town on a run.

name: Sara Machado
about: Sara Machado is a graphic designer interested in branding, sports design, and motion. Her problem-solving mindset and technical approach drive her to meticulously refine her work in both function and form. Away from design, Sara enjoys lots of reading, time in the woods, and exercise.

name: Sarah Gissendanner
about: Sarah Gissendanner is a graphic designer interested in spatial, transit wayfinding, and 3D product design. Her multi-media roots drive her to employ a colorful, eclectic and type-heavy style focused on catching and holding the eye of the viewer. Beyond design, Sarah enjoys participating in fandom and doing fabric/sculpture arts.

name: Sarah Pareja
about: Sarah is a multidisciplinary creative designer pursuing her dream of designing visuals and experiences for the themed entertainment industry. Beyond art and design, Sarah is passionate about classic films, collecting CDs and vinyl records, broadway, rollercoasters, haunted houses, and planning trips.

name: Turner Toliuszis
about: Turner is a graphic designer, storyteller, and lifelong learner. His recent projects have explored speculative futures through publication design, UI/UX, and iconography. He takes pride in crafting visual narratives that are simultaneously structured and whimsical. In his spare time, Turner can be found voraciously reading and collecting colorful items.
